name: impart
description: A Pulumi package for creating and managing Impart resources.
attribution: This Pulumi package is based on the [`impart` Terraform Provider](https://github.com/impart-security/terraform-provider-impart).

nodejs.packageDescription: A Pulumi package for creating and managing Impart resources.
nodejs.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/impart-security/terraform-provider-impart)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-impart` repo](https://github.com/impart-security/pulumi-impart/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-impart` repo](https://github.com/impart-security/terraform-provider-impart/issues).

> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/impart-security/terraform-provider-impart)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,

comparator.description: Greater than, equal to, or less than (should be one of 'gt', 'lt', or 'eq')

delay.description: In milliseconds, the offset from now() for the time window.

threshold.description: Number of occurrences that need to execute to have this condition be true.

timePeriod.description: In milliseconds, the time span from now until when we should be counting events (for example, 60000 is all events in the last minute).

action.description: Strictly for event type monitors. A slug of the action the monitor is tracking.

actorType.description: Strictly for event type monitors. A slug of the actor type the monitor is tracking.

subjectType.description: Strictly for event type monitors. A slug of the subject type the monitor is tracking.

tag.description: Strictly for metric type monitors. The tag the monitor is tracking.

type.description: The type of monitor (should be one of 'event' or 'metric'

dependsOns.description: IDs of the rule this rule depends on.

dependsOnRuleScriptIds.description: IDs of the rule script this rule depends on.

ruleScriptId.description: The ID of the rule script

assertionType.description: The assertion type of the request.

condition.description:
The condition of the assertion.
Accepted values per assertion type:
**output**: contains, not*contains.
**tags**: contains, not*contains.
**status_code**: equal, not*equal, greater*than, less*than, one*of.
**block**: N/A

description.description: The description of the assertion.

expected.description: The expected value of the assertion. It is a string value, and the format it must satisfy depends on the assertion type: **output**: A string. **tags**: A string. **status_code**: An integer value (e.g., "200") or comma-separated list of integers for oneOf condition (e.g., "200,404,500"). **block**: A boolean value represented as "true" or "false".

location.description: The location of the assertion. Allowed values: req, res. Not applicable for assertion type output.

messageIndexes.description: The indexes of the messages in the test case the assertion applies to.

count.description: The number of times to include the message in the test case.

delay.description: The delay in milliseconds between message iterations.

description.description: The description of the test case message.

postDelay.description: The delay in milliseconds after a set of message iterations.

req.description: A payload sent to the inspector to inspect an HTTP request.

res.description: A payload sent to the inspector to inspect an HTTP response.

body.description: The base64 encoded HTTP request body.

remoteAddr.description: The remote address of the request.

truncatedBody.description: Indicates whether the request body was truncated.

body.description: The base64 encoded HTTP response body.

truncatedBody.description: Indicates whether the response body was truncated.

includeQueryParams.description: Include query parameters during spec learning.

includeRequestBody.description: Include request body during spec learning.

includeRequestHeaders.description: Include request headers during spec learning.

includeResponseBody.description: Include response body during spec learning.

includeResponseHeaders.description: Include response headers during spec learning.

learningMode.description: Spec learning mode configuration options. Valid values: all, paths*only, paths*custom. Note: When using 'all' or 'paths*only' modes, the include** flags will be preserved in state but are not applicable as the mode overrides these settings.

provider.description:
The provider type for the impart package. By default, resources use package-wide configuration
settings, however an explicit `Provider` instance may be created and passed during resource
construction to achieve fine-grained programmatic control over provider settings. See the
[documentation](https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/programming-model/#providers) for more information.

impart:index/spec:Spec.description:
Manage a specification.

## Import

The `pulumi import` command can be used, for example:

!/bin/bash

```sh
$ pulumi import impart:index/spec:Spec example "<id>"
```
